county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Shoals. Created 1820 from Daviess and Dubois counties. Martin County is one of Indiana's least populous counties. Crane Naval Weapons Support Center (now Naval Surface Warfare Center Crane Division, 1941) — many families relocated when the military acquired land. Martin County Historical Society. Lost River (subterranean river) area. East Fork White River valley. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Loogootee and Burns City area records. Strong Baptist and Catholic church records. Check Daviess and Dubois for pre-1820 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1820. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1820. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, order books, and judgments from 1820. Indiana Circuit Court is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, bastardy cases, and naturalization petitions (pre-1906) that identify family relationships. Order books often record road petitions naming landowners.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Martin County Auditor, Shoals / Indiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values from 1820. Indiana county auditor records. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Indiana State Archives holds some microfilmed county tax records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Indiana State Archives; others remain at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Martin County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Peru. Created 1832 from Cass County. Peru is the 'Circus Capital of the World' — circus winter quarters records document performers and workers (1880s-1940s). Miami County Historical Society. Significant Miami Indian presence — Peru was home to Chief Richardville (Peshewah), the wealthiest Native American in the U.S. at his death (1841). Miami removal records (1846) at NARA and Indiana State Archives. Some Miami families received land allotments and remained. Wabash and Erie Canal records. Check Cass County for pre-1832 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1832. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1832. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1832. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Miami County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.repository: NARA, Washington DC / Indiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Miami Nation records including treaties, removal records (1846), land allotments, annuity rolls, and agent correspondence. Some Miami families received individual land allotments and remained in Indiana. Chief Richardville (Peshewah) and Meshingomesia's band records. Treaty of St. Mary's (1818), Treaty of Paradise Spring (1826).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records at NARA Record Group 75; some at Indiana State Archives

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wabash and Erie Canal (longest canal ever built in North America, 468 miles) and Whitewater Canal construction records, land purchases, worker rolls, and canal-related property records. Canal workers included many Irish immigrants. Canal land grants at Indiana State Archives.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Canal Commission records at Indiana State Archives; some published by Indiana Historical Bureau

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Bloomington. Created 1818 from Orange County. Indiana University (1820) archives in Bloomington hold extensive collections. Monroe County Historical Society. Bloomington has been a college town since IU's founding. Lake Monroe and Bean Blossom area records. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Limestone quarry records in southern portion. Stinesville and Ellettsville area records. Strong academic community records. IU's Lilly Library holds rare manuscript collections. Check Orange County for pre-1818 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1818. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values from 1818. Indiana county auditor records. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Indiana State Archives holds some microfilmed county tax records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Indiana State Archives; others remain at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Monroe County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.repository: Monroe County Clerk, Bloomington / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court (County Clerk). Post-1906 copies at NARA Chicago. Indiana had significant German, Irish, Swiss, and later Eastern European and Mexican immigration.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Indiana naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry; some at Indiana State Archives

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana provided approximately 197,000 soldiers to the Union Army. Adjutant General records, enlistment papers, and discharge records at Indiana State Archives. Indiana Soldiers and Sailors Monument (Indianapolis). W.H.H. Terrell's 'Report of the Adjutant General of the State of Indiana' (8 vols.) published rosters.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Indiana Adjutant General records well-preserved at Indiana State Archives; indexed online

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Crawfordsville. Created 1822 from unorganized territory (Parke). Wabash College (1832) in Crawfordsville — one of the last all-male colleges in the US; archives hold local materials. General Lew Wallace Study (Ben-Hur author). Montgomery County Historical Society. Sugar Creek valley settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Crawfordsville was an important literary and cultural center in 19th-century Indiana. Darlington, Waynetown, and Ladoga area records. Strong Presbyterian and Methodist church records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1822. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Martinsville. Created 1821 from unorganized territory (Delaware). Morgan County Historical Society in Martinsville. Famous mineral water spas in Martinsville (early 1900s) — health resort records. White River and Indian Creek valley settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Mooresville was the boyhood home of John Dillinger — 1930s records. Brooklyn and Paragon area records. Strong Methodist and Baptist church records. County Clerk holds marriage records from 1822.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1821. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Kentland. Created 1859 from Jasper County — one of Indiana's last counties to be formed. Newton County Historical Society. Kankakee River and Beaver Lake area. Grand Kankakee Marsh drainage converted wetlands to farmland — drainage records document land transformation and settlement. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Morocco and Brook area records. Late settlement means records begin in 1860s. Agricultural county — township records important. Check Jasper County for pre-1859 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1859. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Albion. Created 1835 from Allen and Elkhart counties. Noble County Historical Society. Chain O'Lakes State Park area — numerous lakes attract settlers. Elkhart River and county lake settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Kendallville is the largest city — railroad junction with strong employment records. Ligonier has a Jewish community heritage dating to the 1850s — synagogue records. Rome City and Cromwell area records. Check Allen and Elkhart for pre-1835 records. Gene Stratton-Porter State Historic Site.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1835. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and African American Records — Noble County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Archives / Indiana Historical Society / Levi Coffin House
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana was a free state but had Black Codes restricting African American settlement. Court records include freedom suits, fugitive slave cases, and Black Code enforcement. Levi Coffin of Fountain City (Wayne County) was the 'President of the Underground Railroad.' Quaker meeting records document abolitionist activity.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Indiana State Archives; some published accounts

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Rising Sun. Created 1844 from Dearborn County — Indiana's smallest county in area. Rising Sun on the Ohio River. Ohio County Historical Society. Steamboat and ferry records important for this river county. Rising Sun Casino area. Very small population means limited records, but well-preserved. Cross-reference with Boone County, KY across the river. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Strong Baptist and Methodist church records. Check Dearborn County for pre-1844 records. Aberdeen and Hartford area records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1844. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.source_name: Ohio River Commerce Records — Ohio County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Archives / Local historical societies / Kentucky river communities
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Steamboat records, ferry licenses, river pilot records, and flatboat commerce. Ohio River counties had close economic ties to Kentucky — cross-reference Kentucky county records across the river. Early Indiana settlement came primarily via the Ohio River.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Ferry licenses at county level; steamboat records at NARA and historical societies

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Paoli. Created 1816 from Knox and Washington — one of the original counties at statehood. Orange County Historical Society in Paoli. Paoli was named for Pasquale Paoli, Corsican patriot. West Baden Springs Hotel and French Lick Springs Hotel — resort era records document workers and visitors. Lost River area. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Strong Baptist, Methodist, and Christian Church records. Orleans and French Lick area records. Pluto Water bottling company records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1816. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Spencer. Created 1818 from Daviess and Sullivan counties. Owen County Historical Society in Spencer. White River and Raccoon Creek valley settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. McCormick's Creek State Park (Indiana's first state park, 1916) area. Owen County had significant clay and coal resources — mine and factory records. Spencer and Gosport area records. Strong Methodist and Baptist church records. Check Daviess and Sullivan for pre-1818 records. Relatively rural county with scattered settlements.
